title: References to Information Warfare
summary: Drop #19 described modern conflict in terms of information warfare and psychological influence rather than traditional military operations.
raw_text:
Why is information control important?
Why is propaganda effective?
Who controls the flow of information?
Q

lines.fact: Governments, organizations, and media systems have historically used propaganda and persuasion techniques to influence public opinion.
lines.theory: Q followers interpreted the line as evidence of centralized manipulation by political and media elites.
lines.context: Modern information warfare includes state-sponsored disinformation, algorithmic amplification, and coordinated messaging campaigns.
lines.questions: What measurable evidence would demonstrate centralized coordination versus decentralized media incentives?
lines.Deltas: 2020-2025: Public concern over misinformation and algorithmic influence intensified globally.
